updates.tweet_text:
Untradable grind up continues in #ES_F. Last entry was the 7516 Failed Breakdown Thursday. 7516-7565 was the range. When ranges break, we trend for 1-3 days. 7620 was next target, hit exact.

Plan: Ride runner. 7604 (watch traps), 7585=supports.  7620-24, 7631, 7642 next targets https://t.co/eb3gtSbV8R
updates.notes: 7631 and 7642 named as 'next targets' (new, not in morning plan); 7604 named as watch-for-traps bear level (new). 7585/7620-24 are morning plan reaffirmations.

updates.tweet_text: @MoBKNY Yes 100% ES. Nasdaq is considerably harder and much more random. My win rate wouldn't be nearly as high in NQ
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about trading instrument preference (ES vs NQ). No prices, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @repetak I only take entries that I believe are nearly 100% win rate. That means I need to wait sometimes 2-3 days for a trade. Not everyone the same as me though so I give entries out for people who want to engage more frequently than me
updates.notes: No prices mentioned at all; purely a conversational reply about trading philosophy and frequency of entries.

updates.tweet_text:
Finally getting a little vol burst in #ES_F. No entries since the Thursday 7516 Failed Breakdown that got us long. 7620 was target and after +105 points, we hit it and dipped.

Just arrived at 7585 1st support. 7604 must reclaim to pop. 7574 (watch traps), 7565 below. Sell below https://t.co/wEgCtJNeO2
updates.notes: 7585, 7604, 7574, 7565 all reaffirmations or observations of price action; 7604 not in plan but framed as reclaim observation, not a newly named actionable level.

updates.tweet_text: @Brian_Keith_Pho @repetak Exactly! People criticize me because they think my win rate is unreasonable high, but very very few people do what I do. I trade 1 setup, in 1 market, and I'll wait 3 days if I have to for a 10 point scalp at a planned, A+ zone.
updates.notes: No prices mentioned at all; tweet is a conversational defense of trading methodology with no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @tradethetraps Hopefully! These last few days have been terrible conditions, virtually nothing to trade and when we do get setups its maybe 1 then nothing for 2 days. Need red days to make things interesting for day traders
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about market conditions; no prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @Poonz83 @tradethetraps Yes thats a big factor too. Summer trading is basically grind 90% of the time, then we get a volatility burst once every 3 days and if you don't take advantage, out of luck basically
updates.notes: Pure commentary about summer trading patterns; no price levels named or actionable signals given.

updates.tweet_text:

7604 next up (7620 above). Watch for tests of 7565. Real sell only starts<7565 https://t.co/1DgJw4TMLn

updates.tweet_text:
Very low volatility remains in #ES_F as we fill out the new range. As posted, the range is 7565 to 7604, with 7585 being a magnet in the middle. 2hrs ago we swept 7585, recovered, then pinned there. 7604 was next up, here now.

7620 if this can clear. 7585, 7565 remain supports https://t.co/S8sucEvEEV

updates.tweet_text:
There's the tap of 7604 and we've been here for hours in #ES_F. The regime now is that we get brief bursts of volatility which need to be capitalized on as we chop for days after. 7604-7565=the range with 7585 magnet. We swept 7585, rallied to 7604 target

updates.tweet_text:
We got our little volatility burst in #ES_F this morning & its back into rally/chop regime again. 7565-7604 was the range given with 7585 magnet. We swept 7585 as posted this morning, recovered. Targets then became 7604 (hit), 7616 (hit), 7620-24 (hit).

updates.tweet_text: @chrisAn00930062 @ES_Money_Print Its extremely clear. I wrote the newsletter Friday at 4pm. At this point 7585 was just a normal unactionable support. On Sunday at 6pm we held it and set a very clear *significant low*. This is the primary criteria for a Failed Breakdown. We swept that low this AM and recovered.
updates.notes: 7585 is in morning plan; tweet is hindsight explanation of FBD criteria already triggered, not a new actionable level.

updates.tweet_text: @chrisAn00930062 @ES_Money_Print At 7585 at 6pm Sunday we set a low and rallied to 7620. That would meet criteria 1 (being the Sunday daily low) and 2. That makes it an A+ Failed Breakdown https://t.co/IDURhbdXo5
updates.notes: 7585 and 7620 are both in morning plan; tweet is a hindsight explanation of past price action criteria, not a new actionable level.

updates.tweet_text: @KnightTrader999 @chrisAn00930062 @ES_Money_Print 7604 was the simply the first available entry up when I was posting. I'll include lower quality reclaims here though

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: We got our 20 minutes of volatility burst this morning and as always, it setup a Failed Breakdown of 7585 (Sunday opening low) then probably days of grind again. 7605 now support. Newsletter out soon with next entries from here
